key_insight: The paper demonstrates deterministic, site-selective direct writing of individual perovskite quantum dots with single-emitter resolution, enabling on-demand quantum light sources with <25 nm placement control.
engineering_relevance: This approach provides a practical route to integrate high-performance single-photon emitters with photonic structures, helping overcome the stochastic-distribution bottleneck in quantum-device fabrication.
method: Thermal scanning probe creates a nanoscale confined formation volume for direct QD growth.
Perovskite CsPbI3 emitters are synthesized with spectral tunability and single-atomic-scale placement.
Emitters are coupled to photonic cavities to test deterministic integration and emission enhancement.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports high-yield arrays of CsPbI3 single-photon emitters with narrow linewidths and up to 98% single-photon purity at room temperature, plus enhanced spontaneous emission when coupled to cavities.
watch_item: The approach likely depends on specialized thermal scanning probe control and material-specific growth conditions, which may limit throughput or transferability to other emitter systems.
paper_title: Direct writing of individual quantum dots
